title: Message Envelopes, Bytes, and Schema Evolution · Execution Trace: Buffer Decoding Boundary
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must bound bytes before decoding, validate content metadata, preserve unknown-field policy, and test old/new schema cases; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the buffer decoding boundary increment in a bounded Go envelope decoder and compatibility matrix. Learne…
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Buffer Decoding Boundary · Theory Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 5114 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
